title: Construct Tree from Preorder and Inorder
description:
Given preorder and inorder traversal arrays of a binary tree with unique values, reconstruct the original tree.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
- All values unique
explanation:
Intuition
The first preorder value is the root. Its position in inorder splits the arrays into left and right subtrees. Recurse with index bounds.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Build value→index map from inorder
2. Main Algorithm: Advance a preorder index as we create roots
3. Return Result: Split by inorder positions and recurse

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(n) for hashmap + O(h) recursion

Key Insights
- Index map makes splits O(1)
- Preorder index advances exactly n times

Common Pitfalls
- Slicing arrays causes O(n^2) if not careful
- Forgetting to stop when l>r
